SEARCH
Package minum.testing

Class TestRecordingLogger

java.lang.Object
minum.testing.TestRecordingLogger
All Implemented Interfaces:
ILogger
public class TestRecordingLogger extends Object implements ILogger
This implementation of ILogger is specially designed to assist with tests where something goes awry in a separate thread. This way, when something gets logged about an error condition there, we can easily search for it later in our test. Anything that is logged with this class will get stored into a publicly-available field, "loggedMessages", which you can inspect afterwards.

    void
    logAsyncError(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
    Use this particularly for those cases where we want to log an error but we're stuck inside an asynchronous piece of code, for example, anything unexpectedly bad that happens in ActionQueue
    void
    logAudit(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
    This is for logging business-related topics
    void
    logDebug(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
    For minum.logging data that is helpful to debugging a running system.
    void
    logTrace(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
    this is similar to ILogger.logDebug(ThrowingSupplier) except that it gets run *A LOT*, which can spam up the logs.

      public void logDebug(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
      Description copied from interface: ILogger
      For minum.logging data that is helpful to debugging a running system.

      Parameters:
      msg - example: () -> "Hello"

    • logTrace

      public void logTrace(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
      Description copied from interface: ILogger
      this is similar to ILogger.logDebug(ThrowingSupplier) except that it gets run *A LOT*, which can spam up the logs. Not to mention, that can have performance impacts. By default, we won't show trace information unless requested.
      Specified by:
      logTrace in interface ILogger
      Parameters:
      msg - example: () -> "Hello"
